{{Quote|Have you never cared for anyone?|Syayna to Darth Vader|<ref name="Vader's Quest 4">[[Vader's Quest 4|''Vader's Quest'' 4]]</ref>}}
'''Syayna''' was the [[Jazbinan]] [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] of [[Jazbina/Legends|Jazbina]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].

==Biography==
[[File:RaolSyayna-VQ.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Syayna and Raol]]

Syayna's father, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Prepredenko]], sought the aid of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in rescuing her from "bandits." The bandits, however, were really local [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] fighters with whom Syayna wished to join, including [[Raol]]. Skywalker led Prepredenko's soldiers directly to her, and she was put under house arrest. She was released, however, upon the [[Mission to Jazbina|arrival]] of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who imprisoned Prepredenko for supposedly executing Skywalker. With the help of Skywalker and of [[Jal Te Gniev]], Syayna defeated the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces with minimal bloodshed and drove Vader away.

==Behind the scenes==
Syayna's name is similar to the Slavic word ''sjajna'' (сияйна) [sijajna], which has similar meaning in Bulgarian, Croatian, Serbian, etc.: radiant, effulgent, lustrous or brilliant (in feminine gender).

Many names in ''Vader's Quest'' (her only appearance) are actually words taken from the Croatian language and have the same or similar meaning in South Slavic languages.
